differential_diagnoses: Iron deficiency anemia (likely gastrointestinal blood loss)
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction
Chronotropic incompetence related to beta-blocker use
Pulmonary disease related to history of granulomatosis with polyangiitis
next_steps: Complete blood count with peripheral smear and iron studies
Repeat transthoracic echocardiography to assess aortic valve hemodynamics and cardiac structure
Occult blood testing and evaluation for gastrointestinal pathology given age and anticoagulation
Serial ECGs or Holter monitoring to evaluate for intermittent rhythm disturbances
Cardiac catheterization or coronary CT angiography to evaluate for ischemic heart disease
Cardiopulmonary exercise testing or assessment of medication appropriateness (e.g., tapering beta-blockers)

differential_diagnoses: Severe iron deficiency anemia, likely due to chronic occult gastrointestinal blood loss
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction
High-output heart failure secondary to anemia
Chronotropic incompetence related to beta-blocker therapy
next_steps: Obtain complete blood count with peripheral smear and iron studies
Perform repeat transthoracic echocardiography to assess valvular and ventricular function
Measure B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) and thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels
Perform diagnostic esophagogastroduodenoscopy and/or colonoscopy to identify source of blood loss
Perform coronary computed tomographic angiography (CCTA) to evaluate for obstructive coronary disease
Adjust beta-blocker dosage if heart rate remains inappropriately low during exertion
